assertions.evidence: Opens with 'This is the classic Santa Fe climb' and uses natural, direct phrasing throughout — 'zero excuses about the drive', 'go early or plan on some company' feel conversational rather than brochure-style.
Mentions the 9121ft summit elevation but omits distance (~6 miles) and elevation gain (~1900ft). Two of the three required stats are missing.
'The one basically every local has done at least once and most have done dozens of times' directly captures the iconic, every-local-knows-it character of this route.
'Summer heat on this south-facing climb can be brutal — spring and fall are where this route really shines' gives specific, actionable seasonal guidance with a reason (south-facing exposure).
Starts with 'This is the classic Santa Fe climb' — specific and direct, not a generic 'Nestled in...' or 'If you're looking for...' opener.
